title: The Influencer Who Erased Herself
logline: A Miami lifestyle influencer vanishes—and within 24 hours her accounts are wiped, her bank drained, and her locked apartment looks mid-morning normal: door locked, coffee still warm.

setting.primary_location: Miami, Florida — influencer apartment, brand event venues, and a creator-management ecosystem
setting.timeframe: Present day; critical 24 hours after disappearance

premise.victim_profile: Major social media creator (millions of followers) with lucrative brand deals
premise.inciting_event: Creator fails to appear for a scheduled brand obligation; friends find her apartment locked and untouched while her digital footprint is rapidly erased

suspects.name: The Manager
suspects.role: Talent manager who controlled brand access, contracts, and account permissions

suspects.name: The Boyfriend
suspects.role: On-and-off partner appearing in recent content with a murky financial tie

suspects.name: The Brand Rep
suspects.role: Sponsor-side contact pushing a high-stakes launch with strict NDAs

suspects.name: The Rival Creator
suspects.role: Competitor with prior public drama and private collab negotiations

digital_evidence_targets: Creator management emails + brand deal contract excerpts
Bank transfer alerts / account activity timeline
iMessage/WhatsApp DM mockups + voice memo transcript
Stalker-ish fan subreddit / gossip forum threads
Cloud storage share links / password reset notices

spatial_tool.concept: Apartment + building floor plan (lobby, elevator, unit hallway) to reason about access, cameras, and lock timing

trojan_horse.concept: A mundane ‘influencer logistics’ artifact (PR package intake slip / brand courier receipt) with a timestamp that breaks the obvious story
trojan_horse.purpose: Provides a precise time anchor that forces re-evaluation of who could have accessed the apartment/accounts in the key window

tone.genre: Modern missing-person conspiracy with corporate and parasocial pressure
tone.style: digital_lifestyle; influencer culture; brand-safe surfaces hiding messy backchannels
